source-discipline.reason: Crossref cross-check surfaced 31% mismatch rate (18 of 59 rows). Per rule #1, refused to auto-overwrite mismatched fields. The 18 mismatches retained their original DB-recorded metadata and were flagged via metadata_integrity_status=MISMATCH-* for owner review. No inferred metadata written to fields where the canonical source disagrees with the recorded value.

best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: No best-practice synthesis content authored. Session is Phase B mechanical rehabilitation work — Sonnet-appropriate per workplan §1.

adversarial-research-protocol.reason: No gap closures or research-generating claims in this session.

progressive-measurement-probe.reason: No numerical specification values written. Session writes are metadata fields (journal_name, volume, etc.) and integrity flags.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Direct purpose of session: move 35 rows past the rule #10 existence gate (AUTHOR-TITLE-ONLY → COMPLETE × VERIFIED). New metadata_integrity_status=OK provides content-level verification beyond plain existence per rule #10 sub-rule clarification of DR-2026-05-13. Mismatch holds explicitly stay AUTHOR-TITLE-ONLY (ineligible) until owner review resolves discrepancy.

deviations.reason: Initial push of session record (commit 460e02c5) lacked DOCTRINE token in commit subject and lacked paired attestation file. Rule #11(a) treats sessions/*.md as synthesis-bearing requiring both. Corrected in commit 20d23f55 by adding attestation + DOCTRINE-bearing follow-up commit; the prior commit remains in history per last-session precedent of not force-pushing main to fix commit-msg drift.

Reclassified REF-00398 from HOLD-CROSSREF-MISMATCH to MATCH inline (diacritic-only difference Lariviere/Larivière). The reclassification was correct on substance but bypassed the encoded cross-check — the normalizer should strip NFKD combining marks rather than relying on manual override. Corrected in batch 2's probe_ato_verified_pmid.py with NFKD-strip in normalize().

Batch 3 statutory rehab introduced 3 new COMPLETE-STATUTORY rows. REF-00020 (KR 편의증진법) was upgraded to UNVERIFIED-1 via non-EN primary text + EN secondary corroboration only — the V2-manual routing matrix from DR-2026-05-19 allows this, but the cross-check between stored pub_title and standard_number's documented content is weaker for statutory rows than for academic rows (no canonical title-string API; relies on web-search judgment). Documented in DR-2026-05-20 as a known limitation of the statutory path.

bias_direction: Self-authored bias risk on protocol design: the cross-check uses a conservative threshold (any token disagreement on author surname, year-diff > 1, < 3 shared title tokens triggers HOLD). This pushes toward HIGH false-positive HOLD rate. The 31% / 78% / 57% HOLD rates across the three cohorts are suspicious for that reason; some MISMATCH-flagged rows may actually be cleanly upgradeable with a tolerant normalizer. The batch-2 78% rate is the strongest signal (small N=9) and the most-likely-to-overstate-mismatch. Counter-evidence: the diacritic case (REF-00398) was the only confirmed FP across 75 probed rows; the journal-name-as-title and programme-as-standard patterns are clearly real data quality issues, not classifier strictness. The conservative bias is the safer direction for evidence integrity but is bias. Additionally, the session-end skill promotion was authored by the same session that ran the pilot — typical self-authored-DR bias: pull toward formalizing 'the right way' from limited evidence. Three guard-rails partially counterweight: (1) the NFKD-normalizer fix surfaced one FP showing the protocol is not invariably correct; (2) DR's anti-patterns section names the classifier-strictness risk explicitly; (3) acceptance criteria carve out a separate predicate (verification_note timestamp) allowing 236 legacy COMPLETE rows to coexist rather than forcing back-classification.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An independent reviewer would object on three fronts: (1) The methodology finding (note-as-title / journal-name-as-title / programme-as-standard) is over-claimed from a single session's 75-row evidence — three of the seven batch-1 title-only mismatches might be genuine wrong-DOI rather than note-as-title, and the wrong-PMID misattributions in batch 2 (3 of 9 rows) suggest the underlying issue may be wrong-identifier ingest errors more broadly, not a coherent pattern. (2) The skill promotion at session-end is premature: DR-2026-05-19 step 5 said ≥3 cohorts, which was met technically, but the precedent presumed cohorts of comparable size — batch 3 had only 7 rows, batch 2 had only 9. A reviewer would say wait for at least one cohort of ~50+ rows in the statutory or grey track before promoting. (3) The truncated-DOI heuristic (slash-count + length<20) catches 5 of 6 batch-1 cases but is a brittle pattern that may produce FPs on legitimate short DOIs. Counter: (a) the methodology framing is documented as candidate finding requiring next-session corroboration; the DR's pilot evidence section reports yield rates honestly rather than claiming the patterns are settled. (b) The skill is Status: active because the protocol IS in production use this session, but the underlying DR is Status: PROPOSED awaiting owner ratification — the skill's enforcement is Level 2 (surfacing, not blocking), which precisely allows tightening without breaking the eligible-pool count. (c) The truncated-DOI heuristic is captured in the audit script and skill, both falsifiable; future sessions can correct individual decisions and the trail is preserved via metadata_integrity_detail.
